danger_signals.visual_form: Large sprite bosses filling significant screen real estate; Earth, Wind, Fire, Water elemental visual forms
danger_signals.meaning: Mandatory progression threats requiring specific magic spells to damage; failure to obtain corresponding elemental spell makes encounter impossible to survive
Direct damage to life meter; environmental hazard requiring evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Extended vertical drops in level design
danger_signals.meaning: Sustained fall duration decreases life meter; spatial hazard communication through level topology
Proximity to game-over state; visual urgency indicator

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible sprites scattered throughout levels and in treasure chests
safety_signals.meaning: Life meter replenishment; visual markers for resource availability

safety_signals.visual_form: Safe interior locations accessible from main domains
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones for commerce and gambling; respite from active threats
Potential resource caches (money, spells, equipment); reward for exploration

safety_signals.visual_form: Spell items found in environment or purchased at inns
safety_signals.meaning: Power-up availability; essential tools for defeating bosses without spell make encounters unwinnable

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Four elemental forms: Earth Elemental, Wind Elemental, Fire Elemental, Water Elemental
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each domain contains the corresponding elemental boss form; Malkil's manifestation changes per stage
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Each domain named after elemental type; IceFire Mountain as final stronghold location

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Golden artifacts representing each domain; collected as quest objectives
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: One Animal King per domain; artifact return triggers path progression within each level

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Knight warrior identity; IronSword assembly as ultimate power symbol
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Temporal character progression through equipment upgrades, armor, helmets, shields

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level contains hidden magic spell required to damage boss; spell must be located and equipped before boss encounter
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Elemental boss is invulnerable without corresponding spell (Windbane, Blightwater, Firesmite, Earthscorch); boss behavior unchanged but combat impossible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Previously blocked path becomes accessible; level expands to second domain section
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New areas open allowing progression to boss encounter and spell retrieval

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: IronSword piece obtained; visual reward for completion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Domain cleared; progression to next elemental stage enabled

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: IceFire Mountain becomes accessible as final destination
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final boss encounter with Malkil enabled; game culmination available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Low money supply limits equipment purchases; spell availability dependent on treasure chest contents or random gold bubble spawns
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must choose between purchasing spells, equipment, or keys; economic system gates progression

environmental_storytelling.example: Each of four domains visually themed around classical Greek element (Earth, Wind, Fire, Water); environmental art communicates domain identity and boss type
Golden artifacts belonging to Animal Kings serve as visual macguffins; their retrieval and return signals level progression and world-state changes
IceFire Mountain as final location; name combines opposing elements (ice/fire) signaling ultimate confrontation with shapeshifting wizard
Armor, helmets, shields, and weapons purchased through progression visually manifest power growth; character appearance updated through equipment acquisition

environmental_storytelling.technique: Inn architecture as safe space marker
environmental_storytelling.example: Interior inn locations contrast with outdoor domain exploration; visual sanctuary indicating economic and rest functionality

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Invulnerable; player cannot damage or safely remain in encounter room
Vulnerable to spell attacks; defeatable and yields IronSword piece
Standard hostile behavior; projectile attacks; defeatable with sword or spells
Transformed into money; resource conversion utility
Transformed into food; life meter recovery utility

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Affected by Veil of Slumber Spell
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Slowed movement and attack speed; tactical advantage window
Creates upward water column; vertical traversal mechanic enabling high-reach areas

notes: Ironsword uses elemental theming as primary world-state and faction communication system. The four Elemental bosses (Earth, Wind, Fire, Water) serve as both antagonists and level-zone identifiers. Visual communication relies heavily on: (1) spell acquisition as gating mechanism—spells are essential narrative gatekeepers, not optional power-ups; (2) boss invulnerability states that force players to engage with environmental puzzle-solving to locate domain-specific spells; (3) equipment visual progression through purchasable armor and weapons; (4) treasure chests as reward containers communicating exploration incentive; (5) Inn locations as safe-zone visual markers. The game communicates danger primarily through enemy sprite behavior and projectile patterns, with life meter as persistent threat indicator. Safety is signaled through food availability and spell acquisition. Political/factional structure is minimal—primarily Malkil vs. Kuros with Animal Kings as neutral quest-givers. World-state changes are most visibly communicated through domain transitions and path accessibility gating (artifact return unlocks second domain section). The paradigm is action-platforming focused on resource management (money, magic, health) and pattern-based boss encounters where specific spell matching is mandatory.
